title: Mastichodendron rubrocostatum
taxonomy: Mastichodendron rubrocostatum is a species of flowering plant in the family Annonaceae. It is a member of the genus Mastichodendron and is native to Ecuador. The classification of Mastichodendron rubrocostatum is as a flowering plant, specifically an evergreen tree, belonging to the Magnoliids clade. The plant's family, Annonaceae, is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the custard apple family. Mastichodendron rubrocostatum is further classified as a species within the genus Mastichodendron, which is a relatively small group of flowering plants.

intro: Mastichodendron rubrocostatum, also known as red-veined Mastichodendron, is a medicinal plant species belonging to the family Lauraceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are thought to aid in wound healing, reducing fever, and preventing infections. The bioactive constituents of Mastichodendron rubrocostatum include flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Mastichodendron rubrocostatum is commonly prepared as a tea or infused in oil to create topical preparations, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin infections and respiratory issues.
